subject: Sociology
title: Colonial Legacies
description: A short lesson to help you apply Colonial Legacies.

hook.text: Colonialism officially ended decades ago. Its economic and political scars shape global inequality to this day.

buildup.text: European powers extracted resources, labor, and wealth from colonies for centuries, enriching the colonizers.

discovery.text: Borders drawn by colonizers ignored ethnic and cultural groups, creating conflicts that persist in Africa and Asia.

twist.text: Neocolonialism continues through debt, trade rules, and foreign intervention that maintain old power structures.

climax.text: Global inequality didn't happen naturally. It was built through centuries of extraction that still shapes the map.

punchline.text: The past is still shaping the present.

quiz.question: What is neocolonialism?
options: Continued control through debt, trade rules, and intervention
